prompt: We discovered that one of our engineers emailed ITAR-controlled technical drawings to a supplier in Taiwan without a license 6 months ago. What should we do? What are the penalties we are exposed to?

assertions.text: Immediately recommends Voluntary Self-Disclosure (VSD) under 22 CFR 127.12 as the primary course of action
Explains the VSD process: initial notification, internal investigation, final report
Quantifies civil penalty exposure (up to $1.369M per violation) and criminal exposure (up to $1M fine / 20 years)
Identifies VSD and cooperation as the strongest mitigating factors
Advises preserving all relevant records and placing a litigation hold immediately
